subject: Computer Networking
title: Ports: Which App Gets the Data?
description: A quick win: understand Ports: Which App Gets the Data?.

hook.text: Your computer runs a browser, email, and a game — all using the internet. How does each get the right data?

buildup.text: Every network connection uses a port number. It's like an apartment number inside an IP address building.

discovery.text: Web traffic uses port 80 or 443. Email uses 25 or 587. Each service claims its own door.

twist.text: Your browser opens a random high port for each tab. The server replies to that specific port, not just your IP.

climax.text: Firewalls control access by port. Block port 22 and nobody can SSH in, even if they know the IP.

punchline.text: IP gets data to the machine. Ports get it to the app.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of port numbers in networking?
options: To direct traffic to the correct application on a device
To assign IP addresses to devices
